# hletterscriptgen — compact repository map

Purpose: generator framework that turns rights-clean handwritten Hebrew
page scans into per-writer letter-glyph image sets. Code lives here;
generated datasets land in HeOCR/hletterscript.

Layout:
- src/hletterscriptgen/         Python package and CLI.
  - cli.py                      argparse entry point: version | schema | validate | generate.
  - validation.py               JSON Schema loader + Draft 2020-12 validator helpers.
  - schemas/letter_set.schema.json   Packaged letter_set.v1 schema.
- examples/letter_set/          Fixture letter-set documents used by CI to guard the contract.
- tests/                        pytest suite: schema validity, document validation, CLI smoke.
- docs/                         Human-readable architecture, contracts, integration notes, roadmap.
- schemas/                      (none yet — schemas are packaged under src/.../schemas/)
- .github/workflows/ci.yml      Lint-free CI: pytest + CLI smoke on Ubuntu/macOS, Python 3.11/3.12.
- .github/workflows/release.yml Tag-triggered build + GitHub release with sdist/wheel.

Stable public surfaces:
- CLI: hletterscriptgen {version, schema, validate, generate}
- Output contract: letter_set.v1 (one document per writer)
- Example fixture: examples/letter_set/writer_example.json

Upstream / downstream:
- Upstream:   HeOCR/public-domain-hand-written-hebrew-scans (scan-level rights records).
- Downstream: HeOCR/hletterscript (hosts letter-set datasets) → HeOCR/hocrsyngen → HeOCR/hocrgen → HeOCR / HeOCRsynth.

Out of scope here:
- Hosting glyph images.
- Page-scan ingestion / rights curation.
- Synthetic page composition.
- Dataset governance, release assembly, or publication.
